signals.title: USD/AUD — G8 Bilateral Theme + Geopolitical Stress
signals.text: USD outperformance (1W avg +0.563%) vs AUD underperformance (1W avg -1.106%) creates a 1.669% G8 spread, signaling a dominant risk-off regime. The USD is supported by safe-haven demand following stalled US/Iran talks and hawkish Fed bets, while AUD faces a correction from 0.7280 resistance. Despite a -60bp carry deficit for USD (Fed 3.75% vs RBA 4.35%), geopolitical tension and equity weakness favor the Greenback. AUD/USD at 0.7111.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs Retail Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short -19,437 (long% 22.3%) vs retail 86% long — a sharp institutional vs retail divergence at extremes. While retail is fading the move, specs are positioned for further downside ahead of the 27 May RBNZ meeting. If the RBNZ fails to deliver on the priced hike bias, a long-unwind risk for retail and extension for specs targets 0.5800. NZD/USD at 0.5839.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Options Skew + Intervention Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY at 159.004 — 282bp OIS carry (SOFR 3.55% vs TONA 0.73%) keeps the pair bid, but 25d RR at -1.52 (strong put skew) signals the options market is paying a premium for JPY strength/USD downside protection. BoJ Governor Ueda's comments on rising long-term rates and the proximity to 160.00 increase intervention risk. Carry dominates until VIX > 25, but the options skew suggests institutional caution.

signals.title: GBP/USD — Volatility Premium + UK Jobs Shock
signals.text: GBP/USD IV at 11.0% vs HV30 at 7.6% — 3.4pt VRP indicates elevated hedging demand following the drop of 100,000 UK payrolled employees in April. COT LF net long +40,980 (70% long) remains crowded; the energy shock from Iran tensions threatens this positioning. GBP/USD at 1.3396 faces downside risk if the 18 Jun BoE meeting shifts dovish in response to labor market cooling.

signals.title: USD/CHF — Volatility Premium + Carry
signals.text: USD/CHF IV at 12.2% vs HV30 at 7.1% — 5.1pt VRP signals significant protection buying despite a neutral 25d RR (+0.09). The 375bp carry advantage (Fed 3.75% vs SNB 0.0%) supports USD/CHF at 0.7881, but the high vol premium suggests the market is bracing for a sharp safe-haven CHF bid if Middle East tensions escalate further.
